behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch behavior (recursive/subdirectory event propagation), which is OS-level. A mocked watcher can hide failures where the daemon never receives subdirectory events. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it describes filesystem watching only, with no AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon as a real process against real temp input/output directories. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and assert one transformed output appears without restart.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is scoped to dedup logic for same path + time window, which can be exercised at a single object/function boundary with controlled time. Integration/System were considered and rejected because behavior text says "deduplication logic" and does not require real OS events, subprocesses, or network. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because there is no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup component with a known window and fake clock/time provider; submit the same path twice within the window and assert first is accepted, second is discarded; advance time past window and assert acceptance resumes.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate filesystem events. Real failure appears at OS watcher boundary; mocks often emit idealized events and can miss this. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML decision quality and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: With a real watched directory and running daemon process, perform a write pattern known to generate multiple events (e.g., write+rename/flush) and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is an OS/filesystem lifecycle scenario (directory deletion/recreation, inode replacement) while a live daemon runs. In-process fakes can miss real watcher invalidation/rebind issues. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (no model calls, no agent pipeline).
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete the directory, recreate it at the same path, then write a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamps and concurrent arrival timing, which can differ by OS timestamp granularity and event ordering. A pure in-process mock may hide these failures. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML output dependence and no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times, and assert processing/output order matches observed creation timestamps (including tie-handling policy if equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real I/O race between filesystem events and write completion. Mocked dependencies often cannot reproduce partial-write timing and OS flush semantics, so they can pass while production fails. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (no AI/ML step).
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with real watched directory; write a large file slowly/chunked while keeping the descriptor open, trigger arrival, assert no processing during write, then close writer and assert processing starts afterward.
